rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly frames itself as adding dynamic consumer-directed advertising effects to prior pharmaceutical promotion work focused on physician-directed detailing dynamics, and the cited 2005 JMR paper is exactly about temporal marketing-communication effects (learning vs goodwill) in a new prescription category. Given this strong conceptual and domain overlap (same pharma launch/life-cycle setting and dynamic communication mechanisms), the citation is plausibly foundational rather than incidental.
